verses.fhb_text: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of our Father, to the saints who are in Ephesus, and faithful in Christ Jesus:
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Paul's opening preserves Greek naming conventions; Theos resolves to our Father because Christ is distinguished from God in the same clause.

Renderings:
• "by the will of our Father" — Theos with Christ distinguished resolves to the Father per FHB-1.2-1 NT X-of-God clause
• "Christ Jesus" — preserve Greek word order per FHB-1.2-20

verses.fhb_text: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.decision_notes: Standard Pauline epistolary greeting; preserve canonical form across all Pauline letters per FHB-1.2-20.

verses.fhb_text: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has blessed us in Christ with every spiritual blessing in the heavenly places,
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Opens the extended Trinitarian doxology (vv.3-14) — the apostolic threefold benediction pattern Paul builds throughout the blessing.

Renderings:
• "the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ" — preserve Paul's compound naming structure verbatim; the Father is named explicitly
• "in Christ" — preserve Pauline formula per FHB-1.2-20
verses.footnote: Paul's blessing opens an extended doxology running through verse 14 — our Father blesses us in the Son, and the Holy Spirit seals us. The whole shape of salvation is family.

verses.fhb_text: even as he chose us in him before the foundation of the world, that we should be holy and blameless before him. In love
verses.decision_notes: Pronoun continuity from established Father subject in v.3; "in him" antecedent is Christ from "in Christ" of v.3.

verses.fhb_text: he predestined us for adoption as sons through Jesus Christ to himself, according to the good pleasure of his will,
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Huiothesia — adoption as sons — is the eternal purpose verse of the doxology, the Father's heart for family preserved verbatim.

Renderings:
• "adoption as sons" — render huiothesia faithfully; the filial term is the load-bearing claim
• "through Jesus Christ" — preserve Greek order per FHB-1.2-20
• "to himself" — Father drawing children to himself
verses.footnote: Our Father's eternal purpose isn't rescue alone — it's adoption. He set out from before the world existed to make us his children through Jesus.

verses.fhb_text: to the praise of his glorious grace, with which he has graced us in the Beloved — Jesus.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: "The Beloved" is a NT title for the Son; apposition makes the Father-Son distinction clear at the doxology's central naming moment.

Renderings:
• "the Beloved — Jesus" — apposition per FHB-1.2-19 NT Divine Names; ho ēgapēmenos is the Father's name for the Son (Matt 3:17 echo)
• "his glorious grace" — Father's grace, pronoun antecedent established

verses.fhb_text: In him we have redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of our trespasses, according to the riches of our Father's grace,
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Pronoun antecedents shift — "in him" / "his blood" point to Jesus; "his grace" returns to the Father.

Renderings:
• "In him" — antecedent the Beloved-Jesus from v.6
• "through his blood" — Jesus's blood
• "our Father's grace" — clarify by naming Father since the pronoun antecedent has shifted across the verse

verses.fhb_text: which he lavished upon us, in all wisdom and insight,
verses.decision_notes: Pronoun continuity; Father as subject continues from v.7.

verses.fhb_text: making known to us the mystery of his will, according to his good pleasure, which he set forth in Christ
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: "His will" / "his good pleasure" are the Father's; "in Christ" preserved as Pauline formula.

Renderings:
• "in Christ" — preserve per FHB-1.2-20

verses.fhb_text: as a plan for the fullness of times, to unite all things in Christ, things in heaven and things on earth — in him.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The cosmic-Christ summation; preserve Paul's repetition "in Christ" / "in him" for the rhetorical seal.

Renderings:
• "to unite all things in Christ" — anakephalaiōsasthai, sum up under one head
• em-dash before final "in him" — preserves Paul's emphatic clause closure per FHB-9.8-1

verses.fhb_text: In him we have also obtained an inheritance, having been predestined according to the purpose of our Father, who works all things according to the counsel of his will,
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: "Him who works all things" is the Father; FHB names rather than leaving the pronoun unresolved.

Renderings:
• "the purpose of our Father, who works all things" — "him who works all" resolves to the Father per FHB-1.2-1 X-of-God / agent clause
• "the counsel of his will" — Father's will

verses.fhb_text: so that we who were the first to hope in Christ might be to the praise of his glory.
verses.decision_notes: Father's glory; pronoun antecedent established in v.11.

verses.fhb_text: In him you also, when you heard the word of truth, the gospel of your salvation, and believed in him, were sealed with the promised Holy Spirit,
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Spirit's sealing completes the Trinitarian doxology arc — Father blesses, in Christ, sealed by the Holy Spirit.

Renderings:
• "the promised Holy Spirit" — render to pneuma tēs epangelias to hagion in natural English order; "Holy Spirit" per FHB-1.2-7 post-Pentecost default

verses.fhb_text: who is the guarantee of our inheritance until we acquire possession of it, to the praise of his glory.
verses.decision_notes: The Spirit is the guarantee (arrabōn — down payment); "his glory" remains the Father's, closing the doxology with the third "praise of his glory" refrain.

verses.fhb_text: For this reason, because I have heard of your faith in the Lord Jesus and your love toward all the saints,
verses.decision_notes: Paul's prayer begins; "Lord Jesus" preserved per FHB-1.2-20.

verses.fhb_text: I do not cease to give thanks for you, remembering you in my prayers,
verses.decision_notes: Routine narrative; no FHB-distinctive decision.

verses.fhb_text: that our Father, the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, may give you the Spirit of wisdom and of revelation in the knowledge of him,
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Paul's double apposition — "the God of our Lord Jesus Christ" and "the Father of glory" — both name the Father; FHB carries the relational apposition.

Renderings:
• "our Father, the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory" — apposition naming the Person per FHB-1.2-1 X-of-God; preserve Paul's parallel titles
• "the Spirit of wisdom and of revelation" — preserve Paul's relational title for the Holy Spirit per FHB-1.2-7

verses.fhb_text: having the eyes of your hearts enlightened, that you may know what is the hope of his calling, what are the riches of his glorious inheritance in the saints,
verses.decision_notes: Father's calling, Father's inheritance; pronoun antecedent established in v.17.

verses.fhb_text: and what is the immeasurable greatness of his power toward us who believe, according to the working of his great might
verses.decision_notes: Father's power; pronoun continuity.

verses.fhb_text: that he worked in Christ when he raised Jesus from the dead and seated him at his right hand in the heavenly places,
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: Father raised the Son; the three pronouns risk confusion in English, so one "him" becomes "Jesus" for clarity.

Renderings:
• "he raised Jesus" — replace pronoun with name per FHB-7.2-1 (multiple male pronouns in tight succession would obscure who raised whom)
• "his right hand" — the Father's right hand, Ps 110:1 echo

verses.fhb_text: far above all rule and authority and power and dominion, and above every name that is named, not only in this age but also in the one to come.
verses.decision_notes: Cosmic supremacy of the exalted Christ; preserve as written.

verses.fhb_text: And he put all things under his feet and gave him as head over all things to the church,
verses.decision_notes: Father put all things under Christ's feet (Ps 8:6 echo applied to Christ per 1 Cor 15:27, Heb 2:8); pronoun antecedents clear from immediate context.

verses.fhb_text: which is his body, the fullness of him who fills all in all.
verses.decision_notes: The Church as Christ's body; the fullness of him (Christ) who fills all in all.

chapter_notes: Ephesians 1 is one of the NT's most explicitly Trinitarian chapters. The opening greeting (vv.1-2) names the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. The extended doxology (vv.3-14) follows the apostolic threefold benediction pattern (FHB-1.2-12 echo): the Father blesses us in the Son and seals us with the Holy Spirit, with the threefold refrain 'to the praise of his glory' (vv.6, 12, 14) marking each movement. Paul's prayer (vv.15-23) explicitly names 'our Father, the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory' (v.17). Two FHB-distinctive footnotes added: v.3 establishes the Trinitarian doxology arc (pauline_trinitarian_doxology); v.5 anchors adoption as the Father's eternal purpose (adoption_as_sons). Pronoun antecedents shift between Father and Son several times (especially vv.6-7, v.20); names were substituted for clarity at the load-bearing transitions per FHB-7.2-1. All pronouns lowercase per the updated convention.
